message:
🐛 Reject feedback submissions when FEEDBACK_GITHUB_TOKEN is missing (#1387)

Previously, submitting from the Contribute dialog would silently create
an orphaned record in the local database when FEEDBACK_GITHUB_TOKEN was
not configured — no GitHub issue was created but the UI showed success.

Now the API returns a clear 503 error with instructions to add
FEEDBACK_GITHUB_TOKEN to .env. A startup warning also logs when the
token is missing. Added Helm chart support for the token via
feedbackGithubToken values.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -76,6 +76,12 @@ githubToken:
   existingSecret: ""
   existingSecretKey: github-token
 
+# GitHub token for issue creation from the Contribute dialog (requires repo scope)
+feedbackGithubToken:
+  token: ""
+  existingSecret: ""
+  existingSecretKey: feedback-github-token
+
 # Google Drive API key for benchmark data
 googleDrive:
   apiKey: ""

@@ -24,6 +24,7 @@
 #   2. Create a .env file:
 #      GITHUB_CLIENT_ID=<your-client-id>
 #      GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET=<your-client-secret>
+#      FEEDBACK_GITHUB_TOKEN=<your-pat>  (optional, enables Contribute dialog issue creation)
 #   3. Run: ./startup-oauth.sh           (production build, fast load)
 #      Or:  ./startup-oauth.sh --dev     (Vite dev server, HMR)
